title: Continuous Delivery Outcomes, Flow, Feedback, and Evidence · Workflow Trust Lens: Ci Release Hypothesis Done
summary: A civic TypeScript service ships manually, batches risky changes, measures only deployment count, and cannot connect a production incident to source, tests, artifact, approval, or rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci release hypothesis done increment in delivery outcome and evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating.
Interpret deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, recovery time, reliability, and contextual tradeoffs without gaming one metric.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Release Hypothesis Done · Theory Ci Release Hypothesis Done
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory service deploys 6 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Release Hypothesis Done contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name event, workflow, revision, actor, permissions, runner, toolchain, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, user-signal, and recovery boundaries; predict every state and trust transition; review the smallest deterministic workflow or release change; then verify a fault, repair, rollback, and authorized live-system transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Ci Release Hypothesis Done while producing delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example service deploys 6 times per week but cannot connect reviewed source, required checks, immutable artifact, production revision, user signal, abort decision, or recovery. Define value and delivery evidence without gaming one metric. Competency probe: Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating. A defensible solution applies Ci Release Hypothesis Done, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to delivery outcome and evidence map.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-release-hypothesis-done — Define an observable release hypothesis, risk budget, evidence owner, success and abort signals, and done condition before automating.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-3
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-delivery-outcomes-flow-evidence-3
    artifact: delivery-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked ci delivery outcomes flow evidence 3
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: expire the only retained test artifact during incident reconstruction
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      hypothesis: safer-status-release
      source-revision: reviewed-sha
      checks: required-and-observed
      artifact: immutable-digest
      deployment: revision-identity
      user-signal: successful-status-read
      abort-signal: error-budget-breach
      recovery: prior-digest
    verify: review source-to-user evidence chain and calculate lead time failure and recovery boundaries
    failure: Reject deployment count as value, green checks as provenance, or pipeline completion as user outcome.
    repair: Link reviewed source, exact checks, immutable artifact, observed deployment, user signal, abort decision, and recovery evidence.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
